behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (git-diff/age computation + PR-presence check), not a single pure function (MOCK-MISS). Integration catches real regressions in combined state logic using local fixtures. Rejected System: behavior text does not require "live" network/subprocess execution; it specifies detection criteria, which can be reproduced by wiring modules with fixture branch/PR data. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML output or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Wire staleness evaluator with real internal modules and fixture inputs: branch commit ancestry relative to main, commit timestamps around threshold, and PR-state records. Assert stale=true only when all three conditions hold together; include boundary-age case and mixed-condition negatives.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is startup orchestration: session bootstrap + staleness state + warning renderer. Real failure appears when components are wired, so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess behavior in the behavior text; the key risk is missed wiring or wrong payload passed to warning output. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization in-process with a stubbed current-branch state marked stale and assert emitted warning payload includes actionable context (branch name, age, next action hint). Also assert no warning for non-stale state.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to a "local status-classifier function" with deterministic mapping. That is one function/object boundary, no I/O. Rejected Integration: behavior text disqualifies module-boundary dependence by naming a local deterministic mapper. Rejected higher levels: no OS/network/subprocess or AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over classifier inputs (clean state, stale-without-pr state, edge booleans) asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability across runs.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior contains forcing phrase "against live repo and PR state" (REAL-INFRA). Real failures can be hidden by mocks (API/status shape drift, real git state timing, rendering integration under true inputs), so System is minimum. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM judgment; this is deterministic infrastructure/state reporting.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo context with real PR backend state (or live test org/repo) for two scenarios: clean branch and commits-without-PR stale branch. Assert rendered output contains correct classification text in each scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is decision logic across branch state + PR lifecycle status (module interaction), which Integration catches. Rejected System: behavior text does not require live API execution; open/merged states can be represented in contract-faithful fixtures for this specific false-positive rule. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test staleness detector with fixture branch metadata and PR states (`open`, `merged`, `none`). Assert `open` and `merged` never classify as `stale-no-pr`, while `none` can when other stale conditions hold.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real VCS behavior under rebase/squash semantics (REAL-INFRA via git operations/filesystem/subprocess). In-process mocks can hide ancestry/merge-base edge cases (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration: behavior text specifically anchors on rebase/squash hash changes, which are git-history mechanics best validated with real repository operations. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create real git histories in temporary repos: branch diverges, then rebase onto main; separate case with squash merge into main. Run staleness detection after each history rewrite and assert branch is not classified `stale-no-pr` when work is effectively integrated.
